metadata.description: The Service Bus queue name. Queue names are case-insensitive and will always be forced to lowercase.

metadata.name: ttlInSeconds
metadata.description: Parameter to set the default message time to live. If this parameter is omitted, messages will expire after 14 days (the built-in default value in Azure Sevice Bus).

metadata.name: maxRetriableErrorsPerSec
metadata.description: Maximum number of retriable errors that are processed per second. If a message fails to be processed with a retriable error, the component adds a delay before it starts processing another message, to avoid immediately re-processing messages that have failed

metadata.name: minConnectionRecoveryInSec
metadata.description: Minimum interval (in seconds) to wait before attempting to reconnect to Azure Service Bus in case of a connection failure.

metadata.name: maxConnectionRecoveryInSec
metadata.description: Maximum interval (in seconds) to wait before attempting to reconnect to Azure Service Bus in case of a connection failure. After each attempt, the binding waits a random number of seconds, increasing every time, between the minimum and the maximum. Default is 300 seconds (5 minutes).

metadata.name: maxActiveMessages
metadata.description: Defines the maximum number of messages to be processing or in the buffer at once. This should be at least as big as the "maxConcurrentHandlers".

metadata.name: maxConcurrentHandlers
metadata.description: Defines the maximum number of concurrent message handlers; set to `0` for unlimited. Default: `1`

metadata.name: lockRenewalInSec
metadata.description: Defines the frequency at which buffered message locks will be renewed.

metadata.name: timeoutInSec
metadata.description: Timeout for all invocations to the Azure Service Bus endpoint, in seconds. Note that this option impacts network calls and it's unrelated to the TTL applies to messages.

metadata.name: disableEntityManagement
metadata.description: When set to true, queues and subscriptions do not get created automatically. Default: 'false'

metadata.name: lockDurationInSec
metadata.description: Defines the length in seconds that a message will be locked for before expiring. Used during subscription creation only. Default set by server.

metadata.name: autoDeleteOnIdleInSec
metadata.description: Time in seconds to wait before auto deleting idle subscriptions. Used during subscription creation only. Default: `0` (disabled)

metadata.name: defaultMessageTimeToLiveInSec
metadata.description: Default message time to live, in seconds. Used during subscription creation only.

metadata.name: maxDeliveryCount
metadata.description: Defines the number of attempts the server will make to deliver a message. Used during subscription creation only. Default set by server.

metadata.name: handlerTimeoutInSec
metadata.description: Timeout for invoking the app's handler. Default: `0`

metadata.name: publishMaxRetries
metadata.description: The max number of retries for when Azure Service Bus responds with "too busy" in order to throttle messages. Defaults: `5`

metadata.name: publishInitialRetryIntervalInMs
metadata.description: Time in milliseconds for the initial exponential backoff when Azure Service Bus throttle messages. Defaults: `500`

metadata.name: direction
metadata.description: Indicates the direction of the binding component.

metadata.name: route
metadata.description: Specifies a custom route for incoming events.

metadata.name: connectionString
metadata.description:
The Azure SignalR connection string containing Azure AD credentials. This includes the `AuthType=aad` option.
Note that you cannot use a connection string if your application's ClientSecret contains a `;` character.
metadata.example:
- With a system-assigned managed identity: `"Endpoint=https://<servicename>.service.signalr.net;AuthType=aad;Version=1.0;"`
- With a user-assigned managed identity: `"Endpoint=https://<servicename>.service.signalr.net;AuthType=aad;ClientId=<clientid>;Version=1.0;"`
- With an Azure AD application: `"Endpoint=https://<servicename>.service.signalr.net;AuthType=aad;ClientId=<clientid>;ClientSecret=<clientsecret>;TenantId=<tenantid>;Version=1.0;"`

How often the informer fully re-syncs the ConfigMap state from the API
server as a consistency safety net, independent of watch events.
Set to "0" (default) to disable periodic resync and rely solely on
watch events. Set a duration (e.g. "10m") if you want a periodic
full re-read as a guard against missed events.

metadata.name: redisHost

Connection-string for the Redis host. If "redisType" is "cluster" it
can be multiple hosts separated by commas or just a single host.
The port can be included in the host string (e.g. "host:6379") or
provided separately via "redisPort".

metadata.name: redisPort
metadata.description:
The Redis port. Optional: if "redisHost" already contains a port, this
field must either match or be omitted. When "redisHost" does not include
a port and this field is not set, the default Redis port 6379 is used.
In cluster mode, this port is applied to every host in the
comma-separated list.

metadata.name: enableTLS
metadata.description: If the Redis instance supports TLS; can be configured to be enabled or disabled.

metadata.name: insecureSkipTLSVerify
metadata.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (insecure). Only use for testing.

metadata.name: clientCert
metadata.description: Client certificate for Redis host. No Default. Can be secretKeyRef to use a secret reference

metadata.name: clientKey
metadata.description: Client key for Redis host. No Default. Can be secretKeyRef to use a secret reference

metadata.name: redisMaxRetries
metadata.description: Maximum number of retries before giving up.

metadata.name: redisMinRetryInterval
metadata.description:
Minimum backoff for Redis commands between each retry.
"-1" disables backoff.

metadata.name: redisMaxRetryInterval
metadata.description:
Maximum backoff for Redis commands between each retry.
"-1" disables backoff.

metadata.name: failover
metadata.description:
Enables failover configuration. It requires "sentinelMasterName" to
be set, and "redisHost" to be the sentinel host address.

metadata.name: sentinelMasterName
metadata.description: The Redis sentinel master name. Required when "failover" is enabled.

metadata.name: redisDB
metadata.description:
Database selected after connecting to Redis. If "redisType" is "cluster"
this option is ignored.

metadata.name: redisType
metadata.description: Redis service type. Set to "node" for single-node mode, or "cluster" for Redis Cluster.

metadata.name: dialTimeout
metadata.description: Dial timeout for establishing new connections.

metadata.name: readTimeout
metadata.description:
Timeout for socket reads. If reached, Redis commands will fail with a
timeout instead of blocking. Use "-1" for no timeout.

metadata.name: writeTimeout
metadata.description:
Timeout for socket writes. If reached, Redis commands will fail with
a timeout instead of blocking. Defaults to "readTimeout".

metadata.name: poolSize
metadata.description:
Maximum number of socket connections. Default is 10 connections per
every CPU as reported by runtime.NumCPU.

metadata.name: poolTimeout
metadata.description:
Amount of time client waits for a connection if all connections are busy
before returning an error. Default is readTimeout + 1 second.

metadata.name: maxConnAge

Use Sentinel middleware to guarantee the reliability and resiliency of your application.
Sentinel is a powerful fault-tolerance component that takes "flow" as the breakthrough point and covers multiple fields including flow control, traffic shaping, concurrency limiting, circuit breaking, and adaptive system protection to guarantee the reliability and resiliency of microservices.

The Sentinel HTTP middleware enables Dapr to facilitate Sentinel's powerful abilities to protect your application. You can refer to Sentinel Wiki for more details on Sentinel.

wait time expires, the call returns successfully with an empty list of messages.

of messages per a Dapr producer.

publish and consume operations that implement the default Dapr
component retry behavior. The value can be set to any non-negative
float/integer.

metadata.name: allowedTopics
metadata.description: A comma-separated list of allowed topics for all applications. If empty (default) apps can publish and subscribe to all topics, notwithstanding `publishingScopes` and `subscriptionScopes`.

metadata.name: publishingScopes
metadata.description: A semicolon-separated list of applications and comma-separated topic lists, allowing that app to publish to that list of topics. If empty (default), apps can publish to all topics.

metadata.name: subscriptionScopes
metadata.description: A semicolon-separated list of applications and comma-separated topic lists, allowing that app to subscribe to that list of topics. If empty (default), apps can subscribe to all topics.

metadata.name: protectedTopics
metadata.description: A comma-separated list of topics marked as "protected" for all applications. If a topic is marked as protected then an application must be explicitly granted publish or subscribe permissions through 'publishingScopes' or 'subscriptionScopes' to publish or subscribe to it.

metadata.example:

 MIIEvgI...

metadata.name: maxRetriableErrorsPerSec
metadata.description: Maximum number of retriable errors that are processed per second. If a message fails to be processed with a retriable error, the component adds a delay before it starts processing another message, to avoid immediately re-processing messages that have failed

metadata.name: minConnectionRecoveryInSec
metadata.description: Minimum interval (in seconds) to wait before attempting to reconnect to Azure Service Bus in case of a connection failure.

metadata.name: maxConnectionRecoveryInSec
metadata.description: Maximum interval (in seconds) to wait before attempting to reconnect to Azure Service Bus in case of a connection failure. After each attempt, the binding waits a random number of seconds, increasing every time, between the minimum and the maximum. Default is 300 seconds (5 minutes).

metadata.name: maxActiveMessages
metadata.description: Defines the maximum number of messages to be processing or in the buffer at once. This should be at least as big as the maximum concurrent handlers. Default: 1000.

metadata.name: maxConcurrentHandlers
metadata.description: Defines the maximum number of concurrent message handlers. Default: `0` (unlimited)

metadata.name: lockRenewalInSec
metadata.description: Defines the frequency at which buffered message locks will be renewed. Default: 20.

metadata.name: timeoutInSec
metadata.description: Timeout for sending messages and for management operations. Default: 60

metadata.name: disableEntityManagement
metadata.description: When set to true, queues and subscriptions do not get created automatically. Default: 'false'

metadata.name: lockDurationInSec
metadata.description: Defines the length in seconds that a message will be locked for before expiring. Used during subscription creation only. Default set by server.

metadata.name: autoDeleteOnIdleInSec
metadata.description: Time in seconds to wait before auto deleting idle subscriptions. Used during subscription creation only. Default: `0` (disabled)

metadata.name: defaultMessageTimeToLiveInSec
metadata.description: Default message time to live, in seconds. Used during subscription creation only.

metadata.name: maxDeliveryCount
metadata.description: Defines the number of attempts the server will make to deliver a message. Used during subscription creation only. Default set by server.

metadata.name: handlerTimeoutInSec
metadata.description: Timeout for invoking the app’s handler. Default: 60

metadata.name: publishMaxRetries
metadata.description: The max number of retries for when Azure Service Bus responds with "too busy" in order to throttle messages. Defaults: `5`

metadata.name: publishInitialRetryIntervalInMs
metadata.description: Time in milliseconds for the initial exponential backoff when Azure Service Bus throttle messages. Defaults: `500`

metadata.name: consumerID
metadata.description: Set the consumer ID to control namespacing. Defaults to the app's ID.

metadata.name: allowedTopics
metadata.description: A comma-separated list of allowed topics for all applications. If empty (default) apps can publish and subscribe to all topics, notwithstanding `publishingScopes` and `subscriptionScopes`.

metadata.name: publishingScopes
metadata.description: A semicolon-separated list of applications and comma-separated topic lists, allowing that app to publish to that list of topics. If empty (default), apps can publish to all topics.

metadata.name: subscriptionScopes
metadata.description: A semicolon-separated list of applications and comma-separated topic lists, allowing that app to subscribe to that list of topics. If empty (default), apps can subscribe to all topics.

metadata.name: protectedTopics
metadata.description: A comma-separated list of topics marked as "protected" for all applications. If a topic is marked as protected then an application must be explicitly granted publish or subscribe permissions through 'publishingScopes' or 'subscriptionScopes' to publish or subscribe to it.

metadata.description: The GCP private key id. Replace with the value of "private_key_id" field of the Service Account Key file.

metadata.description: The GCP credentials private key. Replace with the value of "private_key" field of the Service Account Key file.

metadata.name: type
metadata.description: The GCP credentials type.

metadata.name: projectID
metadata.description: GCP project id.

metadata.name: clientEmail
metadata.description: GCP client email.

metadata.name: clientID
metadata.description: The GCP client ID.

metadata.name: authURI
metadata.description: The GCP account OAuth2 authorization server endpoint URI.

metadata.name: tokenURI
metadata.description: The GCP account token server endpoint URI.

metadata.name: authProviderX509CertURL
metadata.description:
The GCP URL of the public x509 certificate, used to verify the signature
 on JWTs, such as ID tokens, signed by the authentication provider.

metadata.name: clientX509CertURL
metadata.description: The GCP URL of the public x509 certificate, used to verify JWTs signed by the client.

metadata.name: enableMessageOrdering
metadata.description:
When set to "true", subscribed messages will be received in order,
depending on publishing and permissions configuration.

metadata.name: orderingKey
metadata.description:
The key provided in the request. It's used when "enableMessageOrdering"
is set to true to order messages based on such key.

metadata.name: disableEntityManagement
metadata.description: When set to true, topics and subscriptions do not get created automatically.

metadata.name: maxReconnectionAttempts
metadata.description: Defines the maximum number of reconnect attempts.

metadata.name: connectionRecoveryInSec
metadata.description: Time in seconds to wait between connection recovery attempts.

metadata.name: deadLetterTopic
metadata.description: Name of the GCP Pub/Sub Topic. The topic must exist before using this component.

metadata.name: endpoint
metadata.description:
GCP endpoint for the component to use.
Only used for local development, for example with the GCP Pub/Sub Emulator. The endpoint is unnecessary when running against the GCP production API.

metadata.name: maxDeliveryAttempts
metadata.description:
Maximum number of attempts to deliver the message.
If "deadLetterTopic" is specified as well, "maxDeliveryAttempts" is the maximum number of attempts before messages are moved to the dead-letter queue.

metadata.name: maxOutstandingMessages
metadata.description: Maximum number of messages a GCP streaming-pull connection is allowed to have outstanding

metadata.name: maxOutstandingBytes
metadata.description: Maximum number of bytes a GCP streaming-pull connection is allowed to have outstanding

metadata.name: maxConcurrentConnections
metadata.description: Max number of concurrent streaming-pull connections to maintain

metadata.name: ackDeadline
metadata.description:
Message acknowledgement duration deadline.
Allows users to specify a custom message acknowledgment deadline after which a redelivery of the message will be performed if the message was not acknowledged.

metadata.name: batchingMaxMessages
metadata.description:
Sets the maximum number of messages permitted in a batch.
If set to a value greater than 1, messages will be queued until this threshold is reached, batchingMaxSize has been reached, or the batch interval has elapsed.

metadata.name: batchingMaxSize
metadata.description:
Sets the maximum number of bytes permitted in a batch
If set to a value greater than 1, messages will be queued until this threshold is reached, batchingMaxMessages has been reached, or the batch interval has elapsed.

metadata.name: compressionType
metadata.description:
Sets the compression type for messages sent by the producer.
Compression can help reduce message size and improve throughput for large messages.

metadata.name: compressionLevel
metadata.description:
Sets the compression level when compressionType is enabled.
Higher compression levels provide better compression ratios but require more CPU resources.

metadata.name: publicKey
metadata.description:
A public key to be used for publisher and consumer encryption. Value can be one of two options:
 file path for a local PEM cert, or the cert data string value.
metadata.example: "-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\n<base64-encoded DER>\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----" or "/path/to/key.pem"

metadata.name: privateKey
metadata.description:
A private key to be used for consumer encryption. Value can be one of two options: file path for
a local PEM cert, or the cert data string value.
metadata.example: "-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----\n<base64-encoded DER>\n-----END PRIVATE KEY-----" or "/path/to/key.pem"

metadata.name: keys
metadata.description:
A comma delimited string containing names of Pulsar session keys. Used in conjunction with publicKey
for publisher encryption.

metadata.name: persistent
metadata.description:
Pulsar supports two kinds of topics: persistent and non-persistent.
With persistent topics, all messages are durably persisted on disks (if the broker is not standalone, messages are durably persisted on multiple disks), whereas data for non-persistent topics is not persisted to storage disks.

metadata.name: redeliveryDelay
metadata.description: Specifies the delay after which to redeliver the messages that failed to be processed.

metadata.name: <topic-name>.avroschema
metadata.description: Enforces Avro schema validation for the configured topic. The value is a string containing a JSON object.

metadata.name: <topic-name>.rawschema
metadata.description:
When set to "true", registers the user-provided schema as-is with the Pulsar Schema Registry
instead of wrapping it in a CloudEvents envelope schema. Applies to both Avro (.avroschema) and
JSON (.jsonschema) schema topics. Callers must also publish messages with rawPayload=true to
send events without a CloudEvents envelope. Use this for topics that are intended to exclusively
receive raw domain payloads.

metadata.name: <topic-name>.jsonschema
metadata.description: Enforces JSON schema validation for the configured topic. The value is a string containing a JSON object.

metadata.name: processMode
metadata.description:

In sync mode, messages are handled one at a time in order.
In async mode, up to maxConcurrentHandlers messages are processed concurrently.
Can be overridden per subscription via subscription metadata.

metadata.name: maxConcurrentHandlers

metadata.name: receiverQueueSize

so that the stream is left at a constant size. Defaults to unlimited.
Cannot be used together with streamTTL; only one stream trimming strategy can be active at a time.

metadata.name: streamTTL

This is an approximate value, as it's implemented using Redis stream's MINID trimming with the '~' modifier.
The actual retention may include slightly more entries than strictly defined by the TTL,
as Redis optimizes the trimming operation for efficiency by potentially keeping some additional entries.
Cannot be used together with maxLenApprox; only one stream trimming strategy can be active at a time.

Connection-string for the redis host. If "redisType" is "cluster" it
can be multiple hosts separated by commas or just a single host.
The port can be included in the host string (e.g. "host:6379") or
provided separately via "redisPort".

metadata.name: redisPort
metadata.description:
The Redis port. Optional: if "redisHost" already contains a port, this
field must either match or be omitted. When "redisHost" does not include
a port and this field is not set, the default Redis port 6379 is used.
In cluster mode, this port is applied to every host in the
comma-separated list.

metadata.name: enableTLS
metadata.description: If the Redis instance supports TLS with public certificates, can be configured to be enabled or disabled. Defaults to false.

metadata.name: insecureSkipTLSVerify
metadata.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (insecure). Only use for testing.

metadata.name: clientCert
metadata.description: Client certificate for Redis host. No Default. Can be secretKeyRef to use a secret reference

metadata.name: clientKey
metadata.description: Client key for Redis host. No Default. Can be secretKeyRef to use a secret reference

metadata.name: failover
metadata.description:
Enables failover configuration. It requires "sentinelMasterName" to
be set, and "redisHost" to be the sentinel host address.

metadata.name: sentinelMasterName
metadata.description: The Redis sentinel master name. Required when "failover" is enabled.

metadata.name: redeliverInterval
metadata.description: The interval between checking for pending messages to redelivery. Defaults to \"60s\". \"0\" disables redelivery.

metadata.name: processingTimeout
metadata.description: The amount time a message must be pending before attempting to redeliver it. Defaults to \"15s\". \"0\" disables redelivery.

metadata.name: redisType
metadata.description: Redis service type. Set to "node" for single-node mode, or "cluster" for Redis Cluster.

metadata.name: redisDB
metadata.description: Database selected after connecting to Redis. If "redisType" is "cluster" this option is ignored. Defaults to "0".

metadata.name: redisMaxRetries
metadata.description: Maximum number of retries for Redis commands.

metadata.name: redisMinRetryInterval
metadata.description: Minimum backoff for Redis commands between each retry.\"-1\" disables backoff.

metadata.name: redisMaxRetryInterval
metadata.description: Alias for maxRetryBackoff. If both values are set maxRetryBackoff is ignored.

metadata.name: dialTimeout
metadata.description: Dial timeout for establishing new connections. Defaults to \"5s\".

metadata.name: readTimeout
metadata.description: Timeout for socket reads. If reached, redis commands will fail with a timeout instead of blocking. Defaults to \"3s\", \"-1\" for no timeout.

metadata.name: writeTimeout
metadata.description: Timeout for socket writes. If reached, redis commands will fail with a timeout instead of blocking. Defaults is readTimeout.

metadata.name: poolSize
metadata.description: Maximum number of socket connections. Default is 10 connections per every CPU as reported by runtime.NumCPU.

metadata.name: poolTimeout
metadata.description: Amount of time client waits for a connection if all connections are busy before returning an error. Default is readTimeout + 1 second.

metadata.name: maxConnAge
metadata.description: Connection age at which the client retires (closes) the connection. Default is to not close aged connections.

metadata.name: minIdleConns
metadata.description: Minimum number of idle connections to keep open in order to avoid the performance degradation associated with creating new connections. Defaults to \"0\".

metadata.name: idleCheckFrequency
metadata.description: Frequency of idle checks made by idle connections reaper. Default is "1m". "-1" disables idle connections reaper.

metadata.name: idleTimeout
metadata.description: Amount of time after which the client closes idle connections. Should be less than server's timeout. Default is \"5m\". \"-1\" disables idle timeout check.

metadata.name: ttlInSeconds
metadata.description: Allows specifying a default Time-to-live (TTL) in seconds that will be applied to every state store request unless TTL is explicitly defined via the request metadata.

metadata.description: Indexing schemas for querying JSON objects

metadata.description:
Interval, in seconds, to clean up rows with an expired TTL. Default: 3600 (i.e. 1 hour).

metadata.name: actorStateStore
metadata.description: Use this state store for actors. Defaults to `false`.

metadata.name: outboxPublishPubsub
metadata.description: For outbox. Sets the name of the pub/sub component to deliver the notifications when publishing state changes

metadata.name: outboxPublishTopic
metadata.description: For outbox. Sets the topic that receives the state changes on the pub/sub configured with "outboxPublishPubsub". The message body will be a state transaction item for an insert or update operation

metadata.name: outboxPubsub
metadata.description: For outbox. Sets the pub/sub component used by Dapr to coordinate the state and pub/sub transactions. If not set, the pub/sub component configured with "outboxPublishPubsub" is used. This is useful if you want to separate the pub/sub component used to send the notification state changes from the one used to coordinate the transaction

metadata.name: outboxDiscardWhenMissingState
metadata.description: By setting outboxDiscardWhenMissingState to true, Dapr discards the transaction if it cannot find the state in the database and does not retry. This setting can be useful if the state store data has been deleted for any reason before Dapr was able to deliver the message and you would like Dapr to drop the items from the pub/sub and stop retrying to fetch the state

metadata.name: keyPrefix
metadata.description: Prefix added to keys in the state store.

metadata.name: bulkGetChunkSize
metadata.description:
Maximum number of keys included in each internal SQL query issued by BulkGet.
When the number of requested keys exceeds this value, BulkGet issues multiple
chunked queries sequentially (not in parallel) and merges the results. This
avoids SQL Server's 2100-parameter limit and reduces connection-pool pressure.
Values `<=0` default to 1000; values above 2000 are clamped to 2000.

authenticationProfiles.title: API Key
authenticationProfiles.description: Authenticate using an API key

metadata.name: key
metadata.description: API key for Anthropic, or for the Microsoft Foundry resource when apiType is 'foundry'.

metadata.name: model
metadata.description:
The Anthropic LLM to use. When apiType is 'foundry', this is the Foundry deployment name
(defaults to the Claude model ID, e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7'). Configurable via ANTHROPIC_MODEL
environment variable.

metadata.name: endpoint
metadata.description:
Custom base URL for the Anthropic API. Required when apiType is 'foundry'; for Foundry use
the form 'https://{resource}.services.ai.azure.com/anthropic/v1'. If not specified for the
default apiType, the public Anthropic API is used.

metadata.name: apiType
metadata.description:
The type of API to use. Set to 'foundry' to call Anthropic Claude models hosted on
Microsoft Foundry; otherwise the public Anthropic API is used.

metadata.name: cacheTTL
metadata.description: A time-to-live value for a prompt cache to expire. Uses Golang durations

authenticationProfiles.title: AWS: Access Key ID and Secret Access Key
authenticationProfiles.description: Authenticate using an Access Key ID and Secret Access Key included in the metadata

metadata.name: region
metadata.description:
The AWS Region where the AWS resource is deployed to.
This will be marked required in Dapr 1.17.

metadata.name: awsRegion
metadata.description:
This maintains backwards compatibility with existing fields. 
It will be deprecated as of Dapr 1.17. Use 'region' instead.
The AWS Region where the AWS resource is deployed to.

metadata.name: accessKey
metadata.description: AWS access key associated with an IAM account

metadata.name: secretKey
metadata.description: The secret key associated with the access key

metadata.name: sessionToken
metadata.description:
AWS session token to use. A session token is only required if you are using
temporary security credentials.

authenticationProfiles.title: AWS: Assume IAM Role
authenticationProfiles.description: Assume a specific IAM role. Note: This is only supported for Kafka and PostgreSQL.

metadata.name: region
metadata.description: The AWS Region where the AWS resource is deployed to.

metadata.name: assumeRoleArn
metadata.description:
IAM role that has access to AWS resource.
This is another option to authenticate with MSK and RDS Aurora aside from the AWS Credentials.
This will be marked required in Dapr 1.17.

metadata.name: sessionName
metadata.description: The session name for assuming a role.

authenticationProfiles.title: AWS: Credentials from Environment Variables
authenticationProfiles.description: Use AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY from the environment

authenticationProfiles.title: AWS: IAM Roles Anywhere
authenticationProfiles.description: Use X.509 certificates to establish trust between your AWS account and the Dapr cluster using AWS IAM Roles Anywhere.

metadata.name: trustAnchorArn
metadata.description: ARN of the AWS Trust Anchor in the AWS account granting trust to the Dapr Certificate Authority.

metadata.name: trustProfileArn
metadata.description: ARN of the AWS IAM Profile in the trusting AWS account.

metadata.name: assumeRoleArn
metadata.description: ARN of the AWS IAM role to assume in the trusting AWS account.

metadata.name: endpoint
metadata.description:
AWS endpoint for the component to use, to connect to emulators.
Do not use this when running against production AWS.

metadata.name: model
metadata.description:
The model identifier or inference profile ARN to use. Defaults to Bedrock's default provider model from Amazon.
You can specify either:
- A model ID (e.g., "amazon.titan-text-express-v1") that supports on-demand throughput
- An inference profile ARN for models that require it (found in the AWS Bedrock console under "Cross-Region Inference")

metadata.name: responseCacheTTL
metadata.description:
A time-to-live value for a prompt/response cache to expire. Uses Go duration strings (e.g. "5m", "1h").
The component also supports the legacy key `cacheTTL` via mapstructure aliases.
